mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, generating positive forward returns for the S&P 500 over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show negative mean or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows when FEDFUNDS is below 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 9.0 percent, consumer spending growth collapses and corporate earnings forecasts are downgraded, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 9.0%, or out-of-sample Sharpe ratio for the signal is negative.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized broad-based demand recovery, which translates into positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when either series declines, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample regression.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth above its 12-month moving average signals expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, generating positive equity returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward returns or Rank IC < 0.01 during periods where industrial production growth exceeds the moving average, indicating regime breakdown.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls reflect strengthening labor market conditions that support consumer confidence and corporate revenue growth, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over five trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in > 60% of months with positive payroll revisions, or the signal predictive power vanishes in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation reading exceeding 0.4 percent triggers expectations of premature monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive or neutral when CPIAUCSL rises above 0.4% MoM, or the threshold fails to separate winning and losing months with statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Strong quarterly GDP growth combined with accelerating retail sales confirms robust aggregate demand, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative forward returns when GDP growth exceeds 2.0% QoQ, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production alongside stagnant payroll growth indicates a supply-side contraction that suppresses earnings expectations, generating negative S&P 500 returns over five trading days.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative returns when industrial production declines, or the joint condition shows no predictive edge in walk-forward testing over 3-year windows.
